title: My First Post Ever
excerpt: I have absolute no idea why I'm doing this.
content:
I never had a blog before so be welcome to my first one and also to my first post ever (~maybe ever)! 

 Here I will try to write about random stuff when the time allows me to. I hope you enjoy it and find it interesting, or not actually. Probably a lot of contradiction will happen but I will try to be as honest as possible to my present self when writting not considering my opinions from the past. I will also share some of my project ideas, and half ways there. 

 Since I don't want to use a database for this it will be most likely hardcoded as json files. I might change it in the future if a heartless bad guy gets access to this humble website. So far, so good (first day only and counting). Furthemore, I would like to inform to you scumbag scraping bot that you are not welcome here. 

 Thanks for reading whatever I write, you are a good friend!

title: Banana Post
excerpt: More thoughts and reflections about bananas.
content: Banana is good. I like bananas.

title: Mushrooms and Capitalism
excerpt: What fungi networks can teach us about markets, value, and decay.
content:
DRAFT — scaffold, to be fleshed out. 

 An intro hooking the reader: the moment I started thinking about mushrooms as more than dinner. 

 Outline to cover: 

 - Mycelium as a decentralised network, and the lazy comparison to 'the market' (and why it might be wrong). 

 - Foraging, commodification, and what happens when a wild thing gets a price tag. 

 - Growth for growth's sake vs. decomposition — fungi only thrive by breaking things down. A degrowth metaphor that probably gets away from me. 

 - Closing thought: what we lose when we measure everything in yield.

title: Ausangate
excerpt: Notes from the trail around Peru's rainbow mountain.
content:
DRAFT — scaffold, to be fleshed out. 

 Set the scene: arriving in Cusco, the altitude, the cold, the nerves before the trek. 

 Outline to cover: 

 - Logistics: how I got there, guide vs. self-supported, what I packed and what I regretted packing. 

 - The altitude — soroche, coca tea, and the slow grind above 5,000 m. 

 - The rainbow mountain itself: the hype, the crowds, and whether it lived up to it. 

 - The quiet parts nobody photographs: the lagoons, the alpacas, the herders. 

 - What I'd tell someone before they go.

title: Huaraz Public Hospital
excerpt: An unplanned visit to a public hospital in the Peruvian Andes.
content:
DRAFT — scaffold, to be fleshed out. 

 Open with how I ended up there — the thing that went wrong. 

 Outline to cover: 

 - The walk/ride in, first impressions, the waiting room. 

 - The care itself: the people, the improvisation, what surprised me. 

 - A gringo's reckoning with public healthcare far from home, and the cost (or lack of it). 

 - Gratitude, and what it made me think about systems back home.

title: Electric Guitar
excerpt: Picking up the electric guitar, again.
content:
DRAFT — scaffold, to be fleshed out. 

 Why now, after all this time. 

 Outline to cover: 

 - The gear: what I bought, what I already had, the rabbit hole of pedals and tone. 

 - The practice reality vs. the fantasy — calluses, plateaus, the metronome I keep ignoring. 

 - Songs I'm learning and the one riff I can almost play. 

 - What playing badly teaches you about patience.

title: Wife's Birthday 2026
excerpt: Celebrating another trip around the sun together.
content:
DRAFT — scaffold, to be fleshed out. 

 A short, honest love-letter of a post. 

 Outline to cover: 

 - The plan (and how much of it survived contact with reality). 

 - A favourite small moment from the day. 

 - Something I'm grateful for this year. 

 - Inside jokes that won't make sense to anyone else — keep a couple anyway.

title: Parrilla Uruguaya in Copenhagen
excerpt: Hunting for proper Uruguayan asado in the Danish capital.
content:
DRAFT — scaffold, to be fleshed out. 

 The craving that started it all. 

 Outline to cover: 

 - What a real parrilla means and why Copenhagen makes it hard (the wood, the cuts, the patience). 

 - The search: places I tried, what was close, what was heresy. 

 - The verdict, and whether I just ended up building my own grill on the balcony. 

 - Chimichurri notes, because someone will ask.
